title: Gajar Matar Sabzi with Roti
description: Sweet carrots and green peas in a simple home-style masala, plated with rotis as a complete vegetable-forward meal.
explanation: Gajar-matar is a winter staple in many North Indian kitchens. Treating it as a full plate with roti keeps vegetables at the center of lunch or dinner rather than as a tiny side.
Carrots bring natural sweetness; peas add color and a little protein. Keep the masala light so the vegetables stay the focus.
instructions: Heat oil, crackle cumin, and saute onion until soft. Add ginger-garlic and cook until fragrant.
Add tomato and dry spices with salt. Cook until the tomato breaks down.
Add carrots and a splash of water. Cover and cook 6–8 minutes until carrots start to soften.
Add peas and cook uncovered until both vegetables are tender and the masala clings, 5–7 minutes.
Finish with garam masala and coriander. Serve with hot rotis.
stepNotes: Dice carrots evenly so they cook at the same rate.
Frozen peas go in later than fresh peas; no need to thaw if you cook a little longer.
Avoid too much water—this should be a semi-dry sabzi for roti, not a curry.
A pinch of sugar is unnecessary if carrots are sweet; use lemon if the dish tastes flat.
Double the batch for lunch boxes; it reheats well.

variationSections.title: Vegetable add-ins
items: Add beans or cauliflower florets for a mixed winter sabzi.
Use purple carrots or include a little beet for color.
Stir in chopped spinach at the end for more greens.

variationSections.title: Plate adjustments
items: Millet roti for a gluten-free variation.
Add a bowl of dal when you want more protein.
Serve with kachumber or cut fruit for extra raw produce.
